---
title: "Uncertainty_Direct-Biosolid"
author: "Dr. Chih-Yu Hung"
date: "2026-4-23"
output: html_document
---



```{r data and function}
# --- Packages ----
library(tidyverse)
library(future.apply)

source("Function for N2O EF_Direct_uncertainty.R")

#Environment parameters
Text <- read_csv("Inputs/INPUT-EF_dep_para_texture.csv") %>% #Soil texture
        select(ECODISTRIC, PROVINCE, Inv_Year_ID, Frac_Coarse, Frac_Medium, Frac_Fine)
PPE<- read_csv("Inputs/INPUT-EF_Fixed_Para.csv") # To obtain Ecodistrict level P, PE, topo

DF <- readRDS("Inputs/BiosolidN_corrected.rds") #Data from Change's work

Data_Env <- DF %>%
  left_join(Text, by = c("Ecodistrict_ID" = "ECODISTRIC", "Province_ID" = "PROVINCE", "Inv_Year_ID")) %>%
  left_join(PPE, by = c("Ecodistrict_ID", "Province_ID")) %>%
  mutate(NSE = 1, 
         Nsource = 2) %>%
  rename(Crop_f = Crop_System_ID ) %>% # 1 = annual, 2 = perennial, 3 IMPAST
  mutate(Crop_f = case_when(
         Crop_f == 1 ~ 1, 
         Crop_f %in% c(2,3) ~ 0.19,
         TRUE ~ NA_real_))

Data <- Data_Env %>% filter(BION_corrected >0 ) %>% #ignore 0 fertilizer to speed up.
        rename_at(c(5), ~c("Fert_LCA_kg")) %>%
        na.omit() %>%
        filter(Inv_Year_ID == 2015) # From the manuscript, can change target year for uncertainty analysis

```

```{r cars}
# --- User knobs (you can change these) ----
use_vec <- requireNamespace("vctrs", quietly = TRUE)
n_mc  <- 2000
seed  <- 2026
workers    <- 6         # keep small on Windows (2–4)
batch_size <- 50        # iterations per worker per future
set.seed(seed)

# 95% CI helper: p is the fractional half-width of the 95% interval relative to mu
rnorm_ci <- function(mu, p95) {
  sd <- abs(p95) * abs(mu) / 1.96
  rnorm(length(mu), mean = mu, sd = sd)
}

# --- Core per-iteration worker (does NOT change your logic) ----
run_one_mc <- function(i, Data,
                       ci_list = list(
                         Fert_LCA_kg = sqrt(0.15^2 + 0.46^2), #15% from application, 46% from N concentration
                         P           = 0.10,
                         PE          = 0.10,
                         Topo        = 0.15,
                         Frac_Fine   = 0.10,
                         Frac_Medium = 0.10,
                         Frac_Coarse = 0.10,
                         Crop_f      = 0.305 #Rochete table 10, (25+36)/2 = 30.5
                       ),
                       par_means = list(
                         slope      = 0.00558,
                         intercept  = 7.701,
                         coef_F     = 2.55,
                         coef_M     = 0.49,
                         coef_C     = 0.49,
                         coef_NSEF  = 1/0.645,   # ≈ 1.5503876
                         coef_NS    = 0.84
                       ),
                       par_ci = list(
                         slope      = 0.22,
                         intercept  = 0.11,
                         coef_F     = 0.20,
                         coef_M     = 0.20,
                         coef_C     = 0.20,
                         coef_NSEF  = 0.13,
                         coef_NS    = 0.60 # From Chang's MS, I would say it's 95% CI not SD
                       )
) {
  df <- Data
  print(paste0("iteration = ",i))
  eps <- 1e-9
  
  # --- 1) Stochastic perturbation of data columns (row-wise) ----
  if ("Fert_LCA_kg" %in% names(df)) {
    df$Fert_LCA_kg <- pmax(rnorm_ci(df$Fert_LCA_kg, ci_list$Fert_LCA_kg),0)
  }
  
  if ("P" %in% names(df))  df$P  <- pmax(rnorm_ci(df$P,  ci_list$P),  0)
  if ("PE" %in% names(df)) df$PE <- pmax(rnorm_ci(df$PE, ci_list$PE), 0)
  #df$Precip <- df$P
  #df$Evapo  <- df$PE

  if ("Topo" %in% names(df)) {
    df$Topo <- pmin(pmax(rnorm_ci(df$Topo, ci_list$Topo),0),1)
  }


  if (all(c("Frac_Fine","Frac_Medium","Frac_Coarse") %in% names(df))) {
    Fhat <- pmax(rnorm_ci(df$Frac_Fine,   ci_list$Frac_Fine),   eps)
    Mhat <- pmax(rnorm_ci(df$Frac_Medium, ci_list$Frac_Medium), eps)
    Chat <- pmax(rnorm_ci(df$Frac_Coarse, ci_list$Frac_Coarse), eps)
    s <- Fhat + Mhat + Chat
    df$Frac_Fine   <- Fhat / s
    df$Frac_Medium <- Mhat / s
    df$Frac_Coarse <- Chat / s
  }
  
    if ("Crop_f" %in% names(df)) {
    df$Crop_f <- pmax(rnorm_ci(df$Crop_f, ci_list$Crop_f),0)
  }
  

# --- 2) Sample parameters once per iteration ----
  slope     <- rnorm(1, par_means$slope,     abs(par_ci$slope)     * abs(par_means$slope)     / 1.96)
  intercept <- rnorm(1, par_means$intercept, abs(par_ci$intercept) * abs(par_means$intercept) / 1.96)
  coef_F    <- rnorm(1, par_means$coef_F,    abs(par_ci$coef_F)    * abs(par_means$coef_F)    / 1.96)
  coef_M    <- rnorm(1, par_means$coef_M,    abs(par_ci$coef_M)    * abs(par_means$coef_M)    / 1.96)
  coef_C    <- rnorm(1, par_means$coef_C,    abs(par_ci$coef_C)    * abs(par_means$coef_C)    / 1.96)
  coef_NSEF <- rnorm(1, par_means$coef_NSEF, abs(par_ci$coef_NSEF) * abs(par_means$coef_NSEF) / 1.96)
  coef_NS   <- pmax(rnorm_ci(par_means$coef_NS,   par_ci$coef_NS), 0)



# Bind into the environment where N2OEF_direct evaluates
env_fn <- environment(N2OEF_direct)
if (is.null(env_fn)) env_fn <- globalenv()  # just in case

assign("slope",     slope,     envir = env_fn)
assign("intercept", intercept, envir = env_fn)
assign("coef_F",    coef_F,    envir = env_fn)
assign("coef_M",    coef_M,    envir = env_fn)
assign("coef_C",    coef_C,    envir = env_fn)
assign("coef_NSEF", coef_NSEF, envir = env_fn)
assign("coef_NS",   coef_NS,   envir = env_fn)

# (optional) also bind to the caller's global env — harmless and can help if helpers look there
assign("slope",     slope,     envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("intercept", intercept, envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("coef_F",    coef_F,    envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("coef_M",    coef_M,    envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("coef_C",    coef_C,    envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("coef_NSEF", coef_NSEF, envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("coef_NS",   coef_NS,   envir = .GlobalEnv)

  # --- 3) Run your existing pipeline UNCHANGED to produce Result_Prov_Crop ----
  Result_Prov_Crop <- N2OEF_direct(df)

  stopifnot(exists("Result_Prov_Crop"))

  # --- 4) Append MC and return in original col order + MC at end ----
  nm0 <- names(Result_Prov_Crop)
  Result_Prov_Crop <- dplyr::mutate(Result_Prov_Crop, MC = i) |>
                      dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(nm0), MC)
  message(
  "iteration = ", i,
  "; slope = ", slope,
  "; intercept = ", intercept,
  "; coef_F = ", coef_F,
  "; coef_M = ", coef_M,
  "; coef_C = ", coef_C,
  "; coef_NSEF = ", coef_NSEF,
  "; coef_NS = ", coef_NS
  )
  Result_Prov_Crop
}



# plan + (optional) raise globals size if needed
future::plan(future::multisession, workers = workers)
# options(future.globals.maxSize = 2 * 1024^3)  # uncomment if you hit "globals too large"

# make batches of iteration indices
idx <- seq_len(n_mc)
batches <- split(idx, ceiling(seq_along(idx) / batch_size))

# a batch worker: runs many iterations in one process, so Data is serialized ONCE per batch
run_batch <- function(ii, Data) {
  acc <- NULL
  for (i in ii) {
    res_i <- run_one_mc(i, Data = Data)  # your function unchanged
    acc <- if (is.null(acc)) res_i else {
      if (use_vec) vctrs::vec_rbind(acc, res_i) else dplyr::bind_rows(acc, res_i)
    }
    rm(res_i)
  }
  gc(FALSE)
  acc
}

syst_s <- Sys.time()

# run batches in parallel
parts <- future.apply::future_lapply(
  batches,
  run_batch,
  Data = Data,
  future.seed = TRUE
)
syst_f <- Sys.time()
syst_f - syst_s

# final combine (once)
Result_Prov_Crop_MC <- if (use_vec) vctrs::vec_rbind(!!!parts) else dplyr::bind_rows(parts)
rm(parts); gc(FALSE)

Result_Prov_Crop_MC <- Result_Prov_Crop_MC %>%
  mutate(N2O = Tot.Fert * N2O.IEF)

#Province and Crop system specific
summary(Result_Prov_Crop_MC$N2O) # mean 5.11
saveRDS(Result_Prov_Crop_MC, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Province_Direct.rds")

#Calculate National level results
Result_MC <- Result_Prov_Crop_MC %>%
  group_by(MC) %>%
  summarise(N2O = sum(N2O),
            Tot.Fert = sum(Tot.Fert)) %>%
  mutate(CO2e = N2O*44/28*265,              # kg CO2e
         `CO2e(kt)` = CO2e /10^6) 

summary(Result_MC$`CO2e(kt)`) #median 31.058, mean 38.049
saveRDS(Result_MC, "Outputs/Uncertainty__Direct.rds")

#Calculate the relative range 
CI <- Result_MC %>%
  summarise(
    median = median(`CO2e(kt)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    rel_lower = (lower - median) / median *100,
    rel_upper = (upper - median) / median *100,
    rel_range = (upper - lower) / median *100
  )
#-72.7 % - 245.9%
write.csv(CI, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Range_Direct.csv")





```
